frame_analyses.description: Two instructors present laparoscopic instruments (endoscope/camera head visible). FLS trainer box with green mat and white pegboard visible in background. Trainee in white coat with mask holding laparoscopic instruments.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational demonstration setup with instructor explanation visible

frame_analyses.description: Close-up of laparoscopic instrument controls being demonstrated by instructor. Endoscope with articulation controls visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructor teaching proper handling of instrument controls

frame_analyses.description: Wide shot of FLS trainer box showing peg board with numbered colored pegs (red/burgundy and black circles numbered 1-12). Camera positioned inside trainer showing the internal workspace.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrates task setup and peg arrangement

frame_analyses.description: Laparoscopic view shows grasper holding white foam object/peg above the peg board. Pegs visible with numbered markers. Grasper positioned to place object.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows peg placement technique during task execution

frame_analyses.description: Continued view of grasper manipulating foam peg on board. Multiple numbered pegs visible (positions 8, 7, 5, 6 clearly marked). Object being transferred.
Laparoscopic view showing grasper with foam object near pegs numbered around positions 9, 10, 11, 12. Continued transfer activity.
View showing multiple pegs with markers visible (2, 3, 4, 5, 6 visible). Object being moved by grasper. Task nearing completion phase.
Laparoscopic view of workspace showing pegs with visible wear marks on several (pegs 4, 5, 6, 12, 11, 10 show transfer tracks). Final object positioning visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows completion state with evidence of all pegs transferred

penalties.description: No dropped objects, failed placements, or penalties visible in instructional video

score_components.note: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL video; scoring provided for reference only. This is not a trainee performance assessment.

confidence_rationale: Video content is clearly educational with instructor narration and setup demonstration. Completion time is derived from timestamp progression. However, video shows demonstration/teaching context rather than formal scored performance. Actual performance metrics are difficult to extract definitively due to instructional framing and multiple camera angles.
cannot_determine: Whether this represents a formal FLS exam attempt or educational demonstration
Exact hand dominance of performer (masked, unclear from angles)
Precise spatial deviation in peg placements (camera angle is internal/fixed)
video_classification_notes: This video contains BOTH instructional content (initial setup, instructor explanation, demonstration of instrument controls) AND what appears to be a task performance sequence. The instructor-led narration and educational framing clearly mark this as instructional material rather than a pure performance video.

demonstrated_techniques: Proper instrument grip and control of laparoscopic graspers
Smooth spatial navigation within trainer box
verbal_cues: Instructor guidance on instrument handling visible from context
common_errors_addressed: Proper placement on pegs rather than missed placements
Coordinated hand switching between dominant/nondominant phases

improvement_suggestions: Video is instructional/demonstration in nature — not a performance requiring improvement feedback
final_assessment: This is an INSTRUCTIONAL video demonstrating FLS Peg Transfer task with educational setup and instructor presence. While a performance sequence is visible, the primary content is teaching/demonstration. Score of 196 is provided for reference based on observed completion time (104s, well within expert range of 40-60s for actual performance, though longer due to instructional delays). This should NOT be treated as an official FLS exam score.
